title: cornus capitata subsp. emeiensis
intro: Cornus capitata subsp. emeiensis, also known as the Emei Mountain dogwood, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cornaceae. It is a shrub native to the mountainous regions of China, valued for its medicinal properties and health benefits. The plant contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are used to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote cardiovascular health. The herbal preparation of Cornus capitata subsp. emeiensis is typically made into a tea or decoction, with the dried flowers, leaves, and stems being used to create a medicinal infusion.
